# Confirmation Dialog

Requests a confirmation or acknowledgement through a shared application dialog.

## Contents

- [Installation](#installation)
- [Add the root component](#add-the-root-component)
- [Usage](#usage)
- [Confirmation options](#confirmation-options)
- [Acknowledgement alerts](#acknowledgement-alerts)
- [Callback and concurrency guidance](#callback-and-concurrency-guidance)

## Installation

Add Confirmation Dialog from the configured StackTrace registry.

```shell
npx shadcn-vue@latest add @stacktrace/confirmation-dialog
```

## Add the root component

Render `ConfirmationDialog` once near the application root. The composable uses shared module state, so calls made elsewhere target this root instance. The optional `to` prop changes the portal destination when the dialog must render inside a specific container.

*AppLayout.vue*

```vue
<template>
  <main>
    <slot />
  </main>

  <ConfirmationDialog />
</template>

<script setup lang="ts">
import { ConfirmationDialog } from '@/Components/ConfirmationDialog'
</script>
```

## Usage

Call `confirmDestructive` immediately before an irreversible frontend action. The callback may be synchronous or asynchronous; while it is pending, the selected button displays a processing state, and the dialog closes after the callback fulfills.

```vue
<template>
  <div>
    <Button variant="destructive" @click="destroy">
      Delete
    </Button>

    <ConfirmationDialog />
  </div>
</template>

<script setup lang="ts">
import { Button } from '@/Components/Button'
import {
  ConfirmationDialog,
  useConfirmable,
} from '@/Components/ConfirmationDialog'

const { confirmDestructive } = useConfirmable()

const destroy = () => {
  confirmDestructive(
    'Are you sure you want to delete this resource?',
    async () => {
      await new Promise<void>((resolve) => {
        setTimeout(resolve, 2000)
      })
    },
  )
}
</script>
```

## Confirmation options

Use `confirm(message, callback)` for the common case. Pass a complete object when the title, action labels, cancel callback, or destructive styling must be explicit. Labels should describe the real operation rather than repeat *Confirm* and *Cancel* without context.

*DeleteProjectButton.vue*

```vue
<template>
  <Button variant="destructive" @click="requestDeletion">
    Delete project
  </Button>
</template>

<script setup lang="ts">
import { Button } from '@/Components/Button'
import { useConfirmable } from '@/Components/ConfirmationDialog'

const { confirm } = useConfirmable()

const requestDeletion = () => {
  confirm({
    type: 'confirmation',
    title: 'Delete project?',
    message: 'This permanently removes the project and its saved views.',
    cancelLabel: 'Keep project',
    confirmLabel: 'Delete project',
    destructive: true,
    confirm: async () => {
      await deleteProject()
    },
  })
}
</script>
```

## Acknowledgement alerts

`alert(message, callback)` shows a single acknowledgement action. Use it only when the message must be acknowledged before work continues; ordinary success, warning, and error status belongs inline or in a toast. `alertDestructive` changes the action's visual intent but does not add recovery behavior.

## Callback and concurrency guidance

Handle failures inside asynchronous callbacks and surface a useful error to the user. A rejected callback does not provide built-in error messaging. This component also represents one shared pending dialog; do not issue overlapping confirmation requests because a later call replaces the current request.

Confirmation Dialog is frontend-only and does not authorize an action by itself. Keep permission checks and irreversible operation safeguards at the appropriate application boundary.

> **Compose local decisions with Alert Dialog: [Alert Dialog](https://ui.stacktrace.sk/docs/components/alert-dialog)**
>
> Use Alert Dialog when the confirmation belongs to one component and its trigger, description, and actions should stay declarative in the same template.

Hosted documentation: [Confirmation Dialog](https://ui.stacktrace.sk/docs/components/confirmation-dialog)
